title: anthactinia napalensis
side_effects: Anthactinia napalensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal problems. Ingesting the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic properties. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap or extracts can lead to skin lesions, rashes, and blisters. In severe cases, improper use of Anthactinia napalensis can result in anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention.
